2605.18627.title: Learning Lifted Action Models from Traces with Minimal Information About Actions and States
2605.18627.paper_summary: It has been recently shown that lifted STRIPS models can be learned correctly and efficiently from action traces alone; i.e., applicable action sequences from a hidden STRIPS model. The result is remarkable because the states are not assumed to be observable at all, and yet it is not practical enough as STRIPS actions include arguments that are not needed for selecting the actions. This shortcoming has been addressed by assuming that the action traces come instead from a hidden STRIPS+ model where some action arguments are implicit in the hidden action preconditions. A limitation of this approach, however, is that it assumes that the states are fully observable. In this work, we relax these restrictions and consider the problem of learning STRIPS+ action domains from traces in a more general context where the traces carry partial information about both actions and states. In particular, we formulate algorithms and completeness results for three general cases, all of which assume full observability of selected action arguments. In the first case, no observability of the state is assumed; in the second case, full observability of some state predicates is assumed, and in the third case, local observability of some state predicates is assumed instead. Given a STRIPS+ domain, these results characterize the conditions under which an equivalent domain can be learned from traces. Experimental results are reported.
2605.18627.paper_summary_zh: 最近已顯示，提升的 STRIPS 模型可以僅從行動痕跡中正確且有效地學習；即，來自隱藏 STRIPS 模型的適用行動序列。這一結果是顯著的，因為狀態並不被假設為可觀察的，但這並不夠實用，因為 STRIPS 行動包含了選擇行動時不需要的參數。這一缺陷已通過假設行動痕跡來自隱藏的 STRIPS+ 模型來解決，其中一些行動參數隱含於隱藏的行動前提中。然而，這種方法的限制在於它假設狀態是完全可觀察的。在本研究中，我們放寬了這些限制，考慮在一個更一般的背景下從痕跡中學習 STRIPS+ 行動領域的問題，其中痕跡攜帶有關行動和狀態的部分信息。具體而言，我們為三種一般情況制定了算法和完備性結果，這三種情況都假設所選行動參數的完全可觀察性。在第一種情況下，假設狀態不可觀察；在第二種情況下，假設某些狀態謂詞的完全可觀察性；而在第三種情況下，則假設某些狀態謂詞的局部可觀察性。給定一個 STRIPS+ 領域，這些結果描述了可以從痕跡中學習到等效領域的條件。報告了實驗結果。
2605.18627.author: Jonas Gösgens et.al.
2605.18627.authors: Jonas Gösgens, Niklas Jansen, Hector Geffner
